vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1697

plan.title: fix(render): make roadmap:render -> roadmap:check idempotent (check diffs against the same bytes render emits)

narratives.Description: task roadmap:check reports drift immediately after a successful task roadmap:render from the same pending/ inputs. The --check path re-renders with subtly different logic/inputs than the write path (header/preamble preservation, multi-issue references[] handling, ordering, or whitespace/newline normalization), so the comparison never matches and the task check aggregate wedges on any project whose ROADMAP touches this path. Make check diff against the exact in-memory render the write path produces.
narratives.Overview:
## What to build

Make `roadmap:render` -> `roadmap:check` idempotent: immediately after render, check MUST exit 0.

Structural fix: --check diffs against the SAME in-memory render the write path produces (one shared render function; check = render-to-buffer + byte-compare). Eliminate any divergence in header/preamble preservation, multi-issue references[] handling, ordering, and trailing-whitespace/newline normalization.

## Where

- packages/core/src/render/roadmap-render.ts -- both the write path and the --check path.

## Out of scope

- Do NOT touch packages/core/src/render/project-render.ts (owned by in-flight #1696) or any triage reader (owned by #1698).
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- packages/core/src/render/roadmap-render.ts: route both write and --check through one shared render-to-buffer function; --check compares the on-disk bytes against that buffer, normalizing nothing differently between the two.
- Add an idempotency test: render then check exits 0, including a fixture with a scope carrying multiple issue numbers in references[].
narratives.UserStory: As a deft operator running task check, I want roadmap:check to pass immediately after roadmap:render, so that the aggregate gate is not permanently wedged by a render that disagrees with its own checker.

narrative.Acceptance: When roadmap:render runs and roadmap:check runs immediately after on the same inputs, check exits 0 with no drift reported.

items.title: shared render-to-buffer for write and check

narrative.Acceptance: When --check evaluates drift, it compares against the same in-memory render the write path emits rather than a separately-computed rendering.
When the idempotency test renders a fixture whose scope lists multiple issue numbers in references[], the subsequent check exits 0.

references.title: Issue #1697: roadmap:render output fails roadmap:check immediately after rendering

expected_outputs: render -> check idempotent (check exits 0 immediately after render)
single shared render-to-buffer function for write + check
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Traced to GitHub issue #1697 (repro + root cause) under umbrella #1699, not a spec section.
